goal_16.content.#9.title: Vis title
goal_16.content.#9.subtitle: Vis subtitle
goal_16.content.#10.title: Affordability of a diet depends on cost and income
goal_16.content.#10.subtitle: Cost (PPP $) for energy sufficient, nutrient-adequate and healthy diets
goal_16.content.#10.scenes.#0.text: In 1990, nearly [emphasis: 2 billion people] worldwide lived in extreme poverty – in other words, on less than $2.15 per day.
goal_16.content.#10.scenes.#1.text: Between 1990 and 2019, the number of people living in extreme poverty was reduced by around 66 percent, from 2 billion to around [emphasis: 660 million].
goal_16.content.#10.scenes.#2.text: Over the same period, the world’s population increased by around 2.4 billion people and the number of people with incomes above the international poverty line [emphasis: increased from 3.3 billion to 7 billion]. As a result, the share of the global population living in extreme poverty was reduced from about 38 percent to 8.5 percent.

goal_16.content.#12.text: You can add custom components like Factoids by selecting them at the bottom of the sidebar and clicking on 'Insert component'. This will create a table like this. Then add/edit text:
goal_16.content.#13.title: Baseline scenario
goal_16.content.#13.label: of global 2019 emissions
goal_16.content.#14.text: If countries continue improving energy efficiency and adopting renewables at the pace of the past decade, the annual emissions needed for all countries to reach the three percent target by 2050 amounts to about five percent of global emissions in 2019. This would mean that eradicating extreme poverty only marginally increases the challenge of getting to net-zero by 2050.
